splits.definition: How total time-on-brand and content format (long vs. short) drive conversion and audience compounding for business creators.
Performance marketing returns in 30-60 days; brand marketing compounds over 6-12 months, requiring expanded attribution windows to measure real ROI.

definition: Time on brand and content format shape conversion more than raw piece count; long-form drives more business outcomes than short-form.
rationale: Career Path Decisions, Life Stage Evolution, and Long-Tail Distribution are unrelated domains (career advice, executive judgment, portfolio theory). Attribution Expansion and Long-Term Value belong together on brand/attribution measurement, not on content surface area math.

splits.definition: Deep relationships with a few high-quality channel partners scale better than managing many low-quality ones; replace weak affiliates rather than accumulating them.

definition: Deep relationships with a few high-quality channel partners scale better than managing many low-quality ones.
rationale: Founding vs Joining Playbooks is career-stage advice unrelated to channel management; Venture Capital Strategy is portfolio investment theory in a different domain. The remaining five members coherently address affiliate/channel partner selection and depth.

splits.definition: Gym/membership businesses flag low-attendance members weekly and systematize personal (non-automated) outreach to prevent churn.
Structured reminder sequences and recurring meeting cadences (daily huddles, weekly one-on-ones, BAMFAM scheduling) that maximize show rates and team alignment.

definition: Attendance-based early-warning systems and personal outreach protocols for preventing member churn in subscription/fitness businesses.
rationale: CRM Limitations is a problem-statement fragment (A2 feature-list), not a concept node. The remaining members split cleanly into gym/member retention (domain-specific) vs. general appointment and communication cadence (broadly applicable sales/ops).

canonical_name: Founder Bottleneck in Delivery and Sales

splits.canonical_name: Founder Bottleneck in Delivery and Sales
splits.definition: When a founder is the primary delivery or sales vehicle, the business cannot scale beyond their personal capacity; restructuring delivery or hiring replaces the bottleneck.

definition: Founder-as-delivery or founder-as-sales creates a hard ceiling; the fix is restructuring the delivery model or delegating before the bottleneck blocks scale.
rationale: Business Model Sustainability (franchise market analysis), Podcast Reach Limitations (channel ROI), DM Setter Bottleneck (ops staffing detail), and Sales vs Operations (vague A4 stub — 'current constraint: sales and marketing limited') are kicked as either wrong-domain or rubric failures. The core five members coherently address founder dependency as a scale ceiling.
